title: College Football 27 Review
description:
College Football 27 reviewed by Will Borger on PlayStation 5, also available on Xbox Series X.

College Football 27’s excellent gameplay, largely smart on-field additions, and best-in-class presentation make this a junior season worth suiting up for. Dynasty and Road to Glory’s additions are welcome, but while both modes are better for having them, they also make me feel like I am never doing much beyond living inside of a spreadsheet when off the field. I wish College Football 27 had more space for on-campus life, and some of the monetization stuff is… gross. But what’s here, especially on the field, is still quite good. This season ain’t gonna knock anybody’s socks off. This was a good team last year, and it’s a good team this year. Just be prepared to worship the Evil God of Numbers a bit more than usual. I guess you either die a hero, or live long enough to operate a spreadsheet.

title: The Biggest Nintendo Switch 2 Games Still Coming in 2026 (Summer Update)
description:
The Nintendo Switch 2 is officially in its sophomore year and showing no signs of slowing down. Following major first-party launches like Pokemon Pokopia and Star Fox, the hybrid console is preparing for a massive wave of new releases. From highly anticipated spin-offs like Splatoon Raiders to legendary ports like Elden Ring: Tarnished Edition and Metal Gear Solid: Master Collection Volume 2, we break down more than 40 games coming to the platform before the end of the year.

NVC's Logan Plant goes over upcoming Nintendo Switch 2 games in 2026, including highly anticipated titles like Fire Emblem: Fortune's Weave, Final Fantasy XIV Online: A Realm Reborn, and the return of The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time. Whether you are looking for physics-based multiplayer fun in Heave Ho 2, massive open-world RPGs like Metaphor: ReFantazio, or action-packed franchises like Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4, this video tracks major upcoming Switch 2 game launches, indie hits, and enhanced editions dropping month-by-month.

title: AC Black Flag Resynced’s Pricy Microtransactions Have Fans Outraged - IGN Daily Fix
description:
In today's Daily Fix:
Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced has debuted with a series-high 100,000 concurrent players on Steam. Although reviews have been very good so far, not everyone's happy. Many gamers are shocked at the amount of microtransactions, with some upset that they shelled out extra $$$ for digital deluxe edition of what they assumed would be a content complete version of the game. Ubisoft has heard the complaints, but it's unclear if there will be any price adjustments or "make-good" for those who bought the digital deluxe edition. In other news, Call of Duty: Blacks Ops 1 and 2 have officially been ported to the PS4 and PS5, but don't expect a comprehensive remaster. The folks at Digital Foundry have found the games to be lacking in modern technical flourishes like 4K resolution and a 120Hz refresh rate (the games are capped at 1080p60). And finally, we end the week with a roundup of the latest game releases.

title: The GTA 3 Mission That Reinvented It All - Art of the Level
description:
Few missions in GTA’s history would make such a big and noticeable change to the geography of the host city as the GTA 3 mission, Bomb Da Base Act II, which goes some way to explaining why all 45 seconds of this short, sharp, Tin Can Alley sniper mission remains so memorable.

Bomb Da Base in GTA 3 massively raises expectations for what’s to come, and proved to be a tantalising glimpse at the ever more complex, Bruckheimer-worthy setpieces that would come to define Rockstar’s later games, their narratives, and the subversive mission design that this most Triple-A of Triple-A studios is capable of.

By today’s standards, it’s extraordinarily simple. But make no mistake: with GTA 3, Rockstar invented the modern video game, and with Bomb da Base Act II, GTA reinvented itself.

title: Fallout: New Vegas Dev Working on New Fallout Game at Obsidian - IGN Daily Fix
description:
In today's Daily Fix:
Obsidian is reportedly ditching Avowed 2 to work on a new Fallout game. As part of Xbox's big reset, developer Josh Sawyer, who worked as lead designer on fan favorite Fallout: New Vegas, will head a team at Obsidian focused on the next Fallout. The last mainline game, Fallout 4, was released in 2015. However, Bethesda has released the MMO Fallout 76 and helped produce the acclaimed Fallout TV series for Prime Video. In related news, id Software was not spared in the big Xbox reset, with more than half of its full-time staff laid off. The developer was reportedly working on pitches for a new Perfect Dark game, among others. The team had also recently released the well-received Doom: The Dark Ages. And finally, morale at Bethesda is shattered in the wake of Xbox's recent mass layoffs, with the remaining developers saying it will have a "substantial and cascading effect" on The Elder Scrolls 6.

title: CardBound - Official Trailer (2026) | Pokemon Trading Card Game Documentary
description:
Don't miss the Official Trailer for CardBound, a documentary about the origins of the classic Pokémon Trading Card Game distributed by Prime Video. Witness the investigation that may point to the first Pokémon card ever officially printed in CardBound, streaming on July 10 on Prime Video, Plex, and YouTube.

CardBound is a feature-length documentary that follows a collector’s search to uncover the true origins of the Pokémon Trading Card Game. What begins as an investigation into the earliest Pokémon cards becomes a global journey through collecting history, rare discoveries, controversy, and the people who have shaped the hobby. Along the way, the investigation leads to the discovery of a previously unknown card that may be the first Pokémon card ever officially printed. 

Featuring Logan Paul, Deep Pocket Monster, Duke Dennis, Olympic champion Noah Lyles, and other prominent voices from the Pokémon and collecting communities, CardBound explores why people collect, how history can disappear, and what happens when one discovery challenges everything collectors thought they knew.

CardBound stars Logan Paul, Deep Pocket Monster, Duke Dennis, Olympic champion Noah Lyles, and other prominent voices from the Pokémon and collecting communities. The documentary is directed and produced by Jacob Zanghi.


CardBound is streaming on July 10 on Prime Video, Plex, and YouTube.

title: Tempest Rising: The Veti's Wrath - Official Announcement Trailer
description:
A demo is out now on Steam for Tempest Rising's upcoming new single-player campaign. Check out the latest Tempest Rising trailer for the real-time strategy game for a peek at the single-player story campaign focused on the third playable faction: the Veti. The demo features the first two missions from the upcoming Veti story campaign, as well as the first two missions from the previous Global Defense Force and Tempest Dynasty campaigns. The demo features a peek at the Veti faction on the battlefield in skirmish mode and custom multiplayer games.

The Veti's Wrath expansion for Tempest Rising will feature the complete Veti faction, an 11-mission single-player campaign and full integration in multiplayer, skirmish mode, and custom games. The Veti's Wrath expansion for Tempest Rising will be available in 2026.

title: Godzilla Minus Zero - Official Teaser Trailer (2026)
description:
Returning to zero is not an option. Check out the teaser trailer for Godzilla Minus Zero, an upcoming film that is a follow up to Godzilla Minus One. Godzilla Minus Zero picks up in 1949, two years after the tumultuous events of Godzilla Minus One, and continues the story of the Shikishima family as they face an all-new calamity. 

War reduced Japan to zero, and Godzilla plunged it into minus. Two years have passed since then, and the country has faced agonizing struggles to achieve recovery and finally reclaim its daily life.

Just as humanity managed to cling to a hard-won peace, a new threat strikes, crushing hope in an instant.

There is no third time. Everything ends here.

Ryunosuke Kamiki returns as Koichi Shikishima, the hero who stood against Godzilla’s terror in Godzilla Minus One, and is joined by Minami Hamabe as Noriko Shikishima, who miraculously survived Godzilla’s first attack on Tokyo. Min Tanaka joins the cast in the pivotal role of Kanji Murakami, a biologist carrying deep psychological scars from the war. 

Additionally, Hidetaka Yoshioka reprises his role as Kenji Noda, who fought alongside protagonist Koichi Shikishima in Godzilla Minus One and now battles the new threat as the director of the Disaster Response Bureau. Yuki Yamada returns as Shiro Mizushima, the young crew member of the Shinseimaru who fought on the front lines, alongside Kuranosuke Sasaki as Seiji Akitsu, the captain of the same vessel. Furthermore, Sakura Ando reprises her role as Sumiko Ota, Shikishima's neighbor who now runs a local orphanage, and Miou Tanaka returns as Tatsuo Hotta, the former captain of the destroyer Yukikaze.

Takashi Yamazaki returns as Director, Screenwriter and VFX lead . The film is produced by Toho Studios and ROBOT, with VFX from SHIROGUMI Inc., the film is currently in production.

Godzilla Minus Zero opens in US theaters on November 6, 2026, and it is coming internationally that same week across Latin America, Europe, Asia, Middle East, Australia, and New Zealand. Exact release dates for individual territories will be announced separately. It will open in theaters in Japan on November 3, 2026.

title: How to Install Epic and GOG Games on Steam Deck (and Steam Machine)
description:
If you shelled out the dough to get a Steam Machine or a Steam Deck, you most likely want to get every dollar's worth of value out of your device. A great way to do that is by installing games you've acquired outside of the Steam ecosystem. Whether you want to take advantage of the plethora of free games given away on the Epic Game Store, play storefront exclusives like Alan Wake II, or start a DRM-free digital game collection with GOG, we have you covered. In this video, we’ll give you a detailed guide on how to install Epic and GOG games onto your Steam Machine or Steam Deck.

To get started with this process, you will need to enter the desktop environment of SteamOS and download the Heroic Game Launcher from the Discover store. From there, we will walk you through how to safely log into your Epic Game Store and GOG accounts, navigate the Wine Manager, and download the latest GE-Proton compatibility layer so your Windows games can run smoothly on your Linux-based handheld. We'll also show you how to automatically add your newly installed games to Steam so they are ready to launch as soon as you return to gaming mode.

title: How to Manage Kenway’s Fleet in Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced
description:
One of the best ways to acquire resources in Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is through Kenway’s Fleet. This Sim City-style minigame lets you amass stolen ships and send them on errands on your behalf. If you're wondering how to start capturing vessels, unlocking missions, and earning a steady stream of passive income, this guide breaks down all the moving parts you need to know to get your pirate armada up and running.
 
Mastering Kenway's Fleet requires progressing past the Julien Du Casse mission, storming heavily guarded regional forts to clear the fog of war, and boarding enemy ships to add to your roster. Once unlocked from your Great Inagua mansion or the Jackdaw's cabin, you can assign Schooners, Frigates, Brigs, and Man O Wars to Trade, Scout, Piracy, and Patrol missions. Managing region risk levels, repairing ship health, and upgrading your berths to hold up to ten vessels are essential steps to succeeding. Whether you're hunting legendary ships for better star ratings or unlocking repeatable warehouse heists, these tips will help you maximize your resources across the map.

title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced - PS4 vs PS5 Graphics Comparison
description:
Assassins Creed Black Flag Resynced is a complete remake of the 2013 original featuring a ton of new gameplay mechanics, visuals, a refined combat style, a much more streamlined story.
In this video we’re going to compare Resynched on the PS5 vs Black Flag on a base PS4. Resynched features a ton of improvements under the hood such as multiple graphics modes on both PS5 and PS5 Pro. On PS5 you get Performance which runs the game at an upscaled to 4k resolution at 60FPS, Fidelity with runs the game as a lower speed of 30fps, but looks better overall, and a balanced move with has the same ray tracing capabilities as fidelity, but runs at 40fps if you have a 120hz display. 
On the PS5 Pro, you have the same options but the upscaling to 4k is enhanced by PSSR and the ray tracing features full global illumination for lighting and reflective surfaces. 
Regardless of what PS5 you play on, this game looks outstanding and is a massive leap from the original PS4 version which runs at a native 1080p locked at 30fps.
